name: Gumroad
one_liner: Creator-focused ecommerce platform for selling digital products, memberships, and subscriptions, with a REST API for products, sales, subscribers, and license verification.
access_notes: Any Gumroad account (free to create) can generate an application and access token from Settings > Advanced ('Generate access token'). No paid plan, review, or approval required. OAuth2 apps also self-serve via the advanced settings page (app icon, name, redirect URI).
api_notes: REST API at https://api.gumroad.com/v2/. Resources: products (list/get/enable/disable/delete), variant categories & variants, offer codes (CRUD), custom fields, product/subscriber files, licenses (verify/enable/disable/decrement uses), sales, subscribers, resource subscriptions (webhooks), and user (authenticated creator data). Roughly 20-40 endpoints, so MODERATE.
mcp_notes: No vendor-shipped MCP server found. Community servers exist: rmarescu/gumroad-mcp (products, sales, offer codes, user data) and keithah/gumroad-mcp (subscriptions, sales, products). Both use a Gumroad access token; neither is published by Gumroad.
main_blocker: None significant. Self-serve access tokens make integration straightforward; only minor friction is that OAuth for third-party multi-account apps requires registering an application.

evidence.claim: Gumroad API uses OAuth 2.0 with base URL https://api.gumroad.com/v2/; access tokens obtained via https://api.gumroad.com/oauth/token; resources include products, sales, subscribers, payouts.
Applications are created through the advanced settings page requiring an application icon, name, and redirect URI; access tokens generated from Settings > Advanced.
Community MCP server (rmarescu/gumroad-mcp) provides tools for products, sales, offer codes and user data using a Gumroad access token from Settings > Advanced.
Second community MCP server (keithah/gumroad-mcp) offers lookup of subscriptions, sales, and products via the Gumroad API.

open_questions: Exact total endpoint count and whether all resource-subscription (webhook) event types remain supported; the JS-rendered official API reference at gumroad.com/api could not be fully scraped for a definitive endpoint list.
